title: 2026 Oscars: How One Battle Beat Sinners, Jordan Overcame Chalamet
text: One Battle After Another triumphed at the 2026 Oscars, winning Best Picture and Best Director. The victory capped what industry observers described as one of the most competitive Best Picture races in recent memory. Before the ceremony, conventional wisdom held that One Battle After Another had achieved the perfect awards season run, having won at virtually every major guild and critics group. However, Sinners' late-breaking momentum created genuine uncertainty. The late surge was driven by two factors: Michael B. Jordan's widely praised performance and the film's record-breaking 16 nominations. Jordan ultimately won Best Actor, proving the strength of his performance even though his film didn't win the top prize. Paul Thomas Anderson won both Best Director and Best Adapted Screenplay, ending his long history of Oscar losses. This was Anderson's first Best Director win despite 11 previous nominations. Sean Penn won Best Supporting Actor for his role in One Battle After Another. The film also won Best Editing and the newly established Best Casting award. Both films were Warner Bros. productions, making it a studio victory regardless of which film won Best Picture. The result validated the predictive power of guild awards, particularly the PGA and DGA, which proved highly correlated with the Best Picture outcome.
